repo_prior_art.cite: ThmSmith/doc/API.md §3: PartialID catalogue is empty; Q1 panel entries are regression-contamination results, not representation-distortion bounds

published_prior_art.cite: BeresteanuMolchanovMolinari2012: random-set sharp regions/support-function logic makes the finite convex-hull envelope routine, though not in text/image notation
ImaiNakamura2024TextTreatments: true/internal representation assumptions give the point-regression endpoint; this is the ε=0 corner
Manski1990/BeresteanuMolchanovMolinari2012: once Theorem 1 gives a non-singleton sharp set, single-endpoint invalidity is the standard point-vs-set identification consequence

novelty_flags.label: proposal
novelty_flags.one_line: tier=letter below novelty_target=flagship; the kernel is a routine finite-support random-set/support-function instantiation, not a flagship-level partial-ID contribution.

novelty_flags.label: Theorem 2
novelty_flags.one_line: The exact-recovery regression endpoint is already the point-identification premise in ImaiNakamura2024TextTreatments and related causally sufficient representation papers.

strengthening_paths.suggestion: Replace the finite-support simplex envelope with a genuinely nontrivial distortion model, such as Lipschitz or transport-capacity restrictions, and derive a closed-form or computable sharp bound not already implied by convex-hull extrema.

Strengthen Conjecture 1 into an open-set/generic-class obstruction for a named estimator workflow, with an explicit quoted target claim from ImaiNakamura2025GPI or CorcollAndreuVlontzosORiordanGilliganLee2026.
Add statistical content: confidence regions or rates for the distortion interval when m_P is estimated from high-dimensional learned representations.

recommended_next_step: Do not send this to Stage 0 as a flagship; pivot to a non-routine distortion geometry or inference theorem before redrafting.
